name: United States of America
executive_summary: The United States remains the world's largest economy by nominal GDP and the dominant military power globally. Strong institutional foundations underpin its position as the primary reserve currency issuer and leading capital market. Elevated federal debt levels and geopolitical competition with China represent the most significant medium-term structural risks for investors.
key_changes: Federal Reserve held rates steady at March FOMC meeting
Trade deficit narrowed slightly amid softer consumer demand
Defense budget supplemental passed, adding $15B for Indo-Pacific posture

active_alerts.title: Fiscal Deficit Trajectory
active_alerts.description: Federal deficit projected at 6.2% of GDP for FY2026, above the sustainable threshold. Bond vigilante risk is elevated if term premium rises unexpectedly.

outlook: Growth expected to moderate to ~2.1% in 2026 as monetary policy restriction works through the economy. Labor market resilience supports consumer spending but inflation risks remain asymmetric to the upside given energy price volatility.
investor_implications: USD assets remain the global safe-haven default. Overweight US large-cap equities selectively; be cautious on long-duration Treasuries given fiscal dynamics. Watch the November mid-term cycle for policy risk repricing.

narrative.executive_summary: The United States remains the world's largest economy by nominal GDP and the dominant military power globally.
narrative.outlook: Moderate growth expected in 2026 as monetary policy restriction works through the economy.
narrative.investor_implications: USD assets remain the global safe-haven default. Selective overweight US large-cap equities.
